key_components: generateNip98Token: Creates kind 27235 signed event with method/URL/bodyHash tags
Authorization header: Base64-encoded signed event with 'Nostr' prefix

AC-039.test_coverage: Nip98AuthService.test.ts: 13 structural tests + 3 property-based tests verifying kind, tags, signature, round-trip

AC-040.evidence: created_at set to current unix timestamp; function is pure (no caching); each call generates fresh event

results.evidence: Kind 27235, empty content, 3 tags, valid signature via verifyEvent
Header 'Nostr <base64>', round-trip decode matches event object
No caching; fresh timestamp per call; different timestamps produce different IDs
created_at within 2s of generation time, well within 60s requirement
verifyEvent used in unit tests, uniqueness tests, and property-based tests
Property-based tests with 5 HTTP methods, varied URLs, random hex hashes; 20 runs each
Only imports finalizeEvent from nostr-tools/pure; no app-specific dependencies
All exported symbols used; Nip98TokenResult is return type annotation
finalizeEvent pattern consistent with crypto.ts and profile-event-builder.ts; proper pattern reuse
Round-trip test validates generate → encode → decode → verify cycle; property tests cover diverse inputs
